title: Multilingual NLP and Low-Resource AI
summary: Advancing language technology for the world's underserved linguistic communities — beyond the top 20 high-resource languages.
description: We build multilingual models, benchmarks, and pipelines that work for the long tail of world languages. Our research spans transfer learning, adversarial robustness, cross-lingual generation, and end-to-end systems for low-resource NLP, with particular attention to Caribbean Creoles, African languages, and dialectal English varieties.

title: Information Integrity and Trustworthy AI
summary: Detecting, attributing, and defending against harmful and synthetic content — across modalities and languages.
description: We study how generative AI reshapes the information ecosystem: fake news, hallucination, deepfakes, jailbreaks, and adversarial attacks. Our work develops benchmarks, defenses, and attribution methods that hold up under real-world, adversarial, and multilingual conditions.

title: Fair, Ethical, and Equitable AI
summary: Auditing AI systems for who they help, who they harm, and building the guardrails that close the gap.
description: We treat fairness as a first-class technical and socio-technical concern — not a downstream patch. Our research audits deployed models for disparate impact, formalizes community-led harm specifications, and develops evaluation metrics that jointly balance utility, safety, and equity.

title: Human-Centered AI
eyebrow: AI with people, not for them
summary: Designing AI systems, interfaces, and evaluations that center human judgment, context, and lived experience.
description: We build human-in-the-loop systems, participatory evaluation methodologies, and interactive tools that respect user agency. This includes conversational AI, RAG-based assistants, and agentic workflows designed with — and evaluated by — the communities they serve.
